13524.disabledMessage:
Goblin tradition holds that
their race was created by
a war-god in order to
fight a war against other
gods.

13525.disabledMessage:
(They never name their
god, so whether it is the
same as any of the gods
worshipped by the other
races is unknown.)

13526.disabledMessage:
The legends also speak
of goblins fighting
against goblins during
the wars, so it is likely
that gods other than
their creators used
them as footsoldiers.

13527.disabledMessage:
Although goblins are still
warlike, it is probable
that in earlier ages
they were more
organised than they
are now,

13529.disabledMessage:
especially if they were
under the direct
command of one or
more gods.

13530.disabledMessage:
This is corroborated
by finds unearthed
at various
archaeological sites,
including the large
excavation east of
Varrock.

13531.disabledMessage:
Armour and weapons
have been found,
obviously made to fit
goblins but far more
well-made than their
current equipment.

13533.disabledMessage:
Almost every piece of
ancient goblin equipment
has one of a number
of symbols on it,
and by correlating these
symbols with the
sketchy records that
survive from the
Third Age it has been
possible to identify
twelve distinct goblin tribes
or regiments.

13534.disabledMessage:
The separate identities of
the tribes have long
since dissolved and
no goblins remember the
symbols, although their
generals may still
recognize the ancient
tribal names.

13535.disabledMessage:
In the following pages
I present a list of
the tribes and their
symbols.

15345.disabledMessage: What would you like to make?

15722.disabledMessage: Clad all in the robes for She

15724.disabledMessage: To beseech the guardians we were sent,

15725.disabledMessage: so to the river's source we went.

15726.disabledMessage: Through the cave we had to travel.

15728.disabledMessage: We came upon a great stone door,

15729.disabledMessage: but our priestess knew the score.

15730.disabledMessage: She used the great ancestral key.

15732.disabledMessage: Three great men all hewn of rock,

15733.disabledMessage: the three small chambers they did block.

15734.disabledMessage: The first all made of rock quite black,

15735.disabledMessage: our fighting men went to attack.

15736.disabledMessage: They went with Arrow, blade and spell,

15737.disabledMessage: but the man they could not fell.

15738.disabledMessage: Many men did fall that day,

15739.disabledMessage: while others turned and ran away.

15740.disabledMessage: I had little hope at all,

15741.disabledMessage: as I watched my kinsmen fall.

15743.disabledMessage: and struck a blow which while was deft,

15744.disabledMessage: should give scant harm to one so strong,

15745.disabledMessage: or so I thought, nay I was wrong.

15746.disabledMessage: The rock man fell down with a crash,

15747.disabledMessage: which sent up clouds of dust and ash.

15749.disabledMessage: two more rockmen left to pass.

15750.disabledMessage: Still buoyed on by my first success

15752.disabledMessage: The next man was of rock hued  grey

15753.disabledMessage: and this one next I planned to slay.

15754.disabledMessage: Crushing blows I rained on him,

15755.disabledMessage: but things were looking very grim.

15756.disabledMessage: Not a scratch could I make.

15757.disabledMessage: Anon my limbs began to ache.

15758.disabledMessage: The rockman gave a mighty roar

15759.disabledMessage: so fierce I hurried for the door.

15760.disabledMessage: How to beat that second man,

15761.disabledMessage: to this day I have no plan.

15762.disabledMessage: My hammer never left a dent.

15763.disabledMessage: I never reached whence I was sent.
